for themselves and done nobly by
other peoples children.


Men have made the laws in all
the ages, and women have borne
the races of the world-as meekly
as possible under existing condi-
tions-but men will do better work
with women helping them as moth
ers, wives and sisters, who would
not shun career. She may not be
eager for fame or so-called free
dom, but any sphere she can fill
acceptably it is not possible for her
to disgrace more thoroughly than
her brother has done.


The Prince of Peace will never
reign without the assistance of
women, and for one pray without
ceasing for the suffraget movement.
We cannot agree with Mrs. Bacon
that women have no talent for law
making Neither can we indorse the
extracts from mans love letters;
as it shows him up in the light of
supreme selfishness.


A MOTHER.


Don't Forget Mind.


To the Editor OF The Press: The
expression, "women's rights" is OBJ
jectionable, and word suffrage"
as applied to wOmankind, is hui
initiating. A woman should have
